body:
## What this does

Establishes a single, commercially-coherent **source of truth** that repositions Dealix from a "Saudi B2B Revenue Engine" to a **Saudi AI Business Operating System**, where Revenue OS is the *first commercial wedge* (the **Command Sprint**) rather than the whole identity.

> Widen the vision, narrow the execution, sell the first wedge, then expand from inside the customer.

## New canonical docs

- **`docs/00_platform_truth/`** — Source of Truth, Business OS Architecture, Product Family Map, honest **Module Status Map**, Public Positioning, index README.
- **`docs/01_go_to_market/`** — Wedge Strategy, **Command Sprint Offer**, Price Authority, First 30 Prospects, Sales Playbook, Partner Strategy.
- **`docs/02_operating_systems/`** — specs for all **14 OS modules**, each with an honest `LIVE/BETA/INTERNAL/DOCS_ONLY/FUTURE` status.
- **`docs/03_governance/`** — Human Approval Policy, Claims Register, Data Retention, PDPL Readiness, Security Baseline, External Actions Policy.
- **`docs/04_delivery/`** — Command Sprint Delivery OS + Customer Folder, Proof Pack, Executive Brief templates.
- **`docs/05_founder/`** — Daily Command, Weekly Board Review, CEO Scorecard, 12-month Business Metrics.

## READMEs

- `README.md` — repositioned to Business OS, with a platform-truth link table.
- `README.ar.md` — **fully rewritten** from the old `شركة AI السعودية` identity to the Business OS positioning.

## CI fixes (repository-hardening.yml)

This branch also makes the pre-existing security checks actually runnable:

- **Trivy** — `aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0` (non-resolvable version) → `@master`.
- **Gitleaks** — the action requires a paid org license → swapped to the license-free gitleaks CLI honoring `.gitleaks.toml`.
- **Reviewed false positives** allowlisted surgically (not blanket-suppressed):
  - `.gitleaksignore` — env-var `curl -u "${VAR}:"` refs + a `30 * 86400` TTL literal.
  - `.trivyignore.yaml` — path-scoped `stripe-secret-token` matches in two verify scripts (grep *detection patterns*, not real keys).
  - `tests/` allowlisted in `.gitleaks.toml` (synthetic redaction-test fixtures).

## ⚠️ Known CI state

| Check | State | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| Gitleaks secret scan | ✅ expected green | license-free CLI + reviewed FPs allowlisted |
| Trivy filesystem scan | 🔴 **intentionally red** | Trivy now runs and correctly reports **real** CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs in `next@15.1.3` (`CVE-2025-29927`, `CVE-2025-55182`, …), present on `main`. These are **not** suppressed — they are being fixed via dedicated **dependabot PRs** (next 15.5.x / 16.x). This check will go green once that upgrade lands. |

Real dependency vulnerabilities are deliberately **not** masked to force a green check.

## Scope note

Docs/positioning + CI-runnability only — no application code, schema, or business-logic changes. Module status is declared honestly so nothing is sold above its real readiness.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01X8mKUD4GjJvoHNDuDXf41Q

commits.messageBody:
…nt wedge

Establish a single, commercially-coherent source of truth that positions
Dealix as a Saudi AI Business Operating System, with Revenue OS as the first
commercial wedge (Command Sprint) rather than the whole identity.

- docs/00_platform_truth: source of truth, architecture, product family map,
  honest module status map, public positioning
- docs/01_go_to_market: wedge strategy, Command Sprint offer, price authority,
  first 30 prospects, sales playbook, partner strategy
- docs/02_operating_systems: specs for all 14 OS modules with honest status
- docs/03_governance: human-approval policy, claims register, data retention,
  PDPL readiness, security baseline, external-actions policy
- docs/04_delivery: Command Sprint delivery OS + customer folder, proof pack,
  executive brief templates
- docs/05_founder: daily command, weekly board review, CEO scorecard, metrics
- README.md / README.ar.md: repositioned to Business OS; AR README fully rewritten
  from the old 'شركة AI السعودية' identity

Honors the doctrine: approval-first, no auto-send/scraping, honest module
status, PDPL/ZATCA-aware (not certified), no claim without evidence.
commits.messageHeadline: platform: reposition Dealix as Saudi AI Business OS with Command Spri…

commits.messageBody:
Both checks were failing for pre-existing infra reasons unrelated to the
docs repositioning:

- Trivy filesystem scan pinned aquasecurity/trivy-action@0.24.0, a version
  that no longer resolves -> use @master (consistent with docker-build.yml).
- Gitleaks action requires a paid license for organizations -> replace with
  the license-free gitleaks CLI, honoring .gitleaks.toml.

Allowlist tests/ in .gitleaks.toml: the flagged matches are intentional
synthetic fixtures (e.g. sk_live_AbCdEf..., ghp_aaaa...) used to test secret
redaction and live-charge refusal logic, not real secrets.
commits.messageHeadline: ci: fix repository-hardening secret + filesystem scans

commits.messageBody:
The license-free gitleaks CLI now runs successfully and flagged 3 findings in
pre-existing files. All three were manually reviewed and confirmed NOT to be
real secrets:

- api/routers/pricing.py:250 — idempotency TTL (30 * 86400 seconds), not a key
- docs/ops/moyasar_live_test.sh:40 — curl -u "${MOYASAR_SECRET_KEY}:" env ref
- scripts/ops/moyasar_pilot_test.sh:103 — curl -u "${MOYASAR_KEY}:" env ref

Recorded as reviewed false positives in .gitleaksignore (the rest of each file
remains fully scanned).
commits.messageHeadline: ci: allowlist reviewed gitleaks false positives

commits.messageBody:
The Trivy fs scan now runs and flagged stripe-secret-token in two verification
scripts whose lines are grep patterns that search FOR secrets
(scripts/v10_master_verify.sh, scripts/v7_launch_verify.sh) — not real keys.
Path-scoped .trivyignore.yaml so the rule is not suppressed repo-wide.

Real next@15.1.3 CRITICAL/HIGH CVEs are deliberately NOT ignored; they are
handled via dedicated dependabot PRs (next 15.5.x / 16.x), so the Trivy check
remains red on those until that upgrade lands.
commits.messageHeadline: ci: allowlist Trivy secret false positives (detection patterns)

title: Reposition Dealix as a Saudi AI Business Operating System (Command Sprint wedge)
